factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable, with slight increases in cash and cash equivalents.

evidence.text: At March 30, 2018, our cash and cash equivalents were $835.4 million, an increase of $61.2 million from $774.2 million at September 29, 2017. This increase was due to cash flows provided by operations of $54.2 million and favorable cash flows from financing activities of $1.5 billion, partly offset by $1.5 billion in cash flows used in investing activities.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow is stable with slight increases, indicating good financial health.

factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, driven by higher revenues across all segments, particularly due to the CH2M acquisition and increased customer activity.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues was due primarily to favorable impacts from the CH2M acquisition, which contributed approximately $1.18 billion in incremental revenue for the quarter and $1.31 billion for the six months ended March 30, 2018. Also, higher volumes in our legacy ATEN, BIAF and ECR businesses also contributed to the increase.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, driven by higher personnel related costs and restructuring expenses associated with the CH2M acquisition.

evidence.text: SG&A expenses for the three months ended March 30, 2018 were $627.1 million , an increase of $276.0 million , or 78.6% , from $351.1 million for the corresponding period last year. The increase in SG&A expenses for the three months ended comparative periods was due mainly to incremental SG&A expense from the acquired CH2M businesses amounting to approximately $246.7 million during the three-month 2018 period, which included $48.6 million of restructuring and other charges and deal related costs.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to higher personnel costs and restructuring expenses.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include unfavorable foreign exchange impacts, ongoing restructuring costs, and integration challenges related to the CH2M acquisition.

evidence.text: Also, unfavorable impacts from foreign exchange (mainly from the stronger Euro and British Pound) approximated $10.4 million. factors.summary: Regulatory changes, particularly the U.S. Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, are impacting the company's tax rates and creating additional expenses.

evidence.text: The Act reduces the top corporate U.S. federal statutory tax rate from 35% to 21% starting on January 1, 2018, resulting in a blended statutory tax rate for fiscal year filers. The Company’s blended federal statutory tax rate for fiscal 2018 is 24.6%. It also requires companies to pay a one-time transition tax on earnings of certain foreign subsidiaries, places limitations and exclusions on varied tax deductions and creates new taxes on certain foreign sourced earnings.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Regulatory changes impact tax rates and create expenses, but there's no clear indication of significant positive or negative trajectory.

factors.summary: The company is engaged in ongoing restructuring and integration activities aimed at achieving $150 million in annual cost savings by 2019. While the activities are expected to continue through 2019, there is no specific mention of measurable savings or progress being made towards the target.

evidence.text: These activities are expected to continue through 2019. These activities are not expected to involve the exit of any service types or client end-markets. The Company is targeting to achieve annual cost savings of $150.0 million upon the completion of these activities.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: During the fourth fiscal quarter of 2017, the Company implemented certain restructuring activities (primarily severance related activities) associated with the Company’s announced definitive agreement to acquire CH2M. Following the closing of the CH2M acquisition, these activities have continued into the first half of fiscal 2018 and include associated charges for professional services, personnel costs, severance and costs associated with co-locating Jacobs and CH2M offices, amounting to approximately $69.4 million in pre-tax charges during second quarter ended March 30, 2018. These activities are expected to continue through 2019.
evidence.subsection: Segment Financial Information

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing cost-saving activities without specific progress toward the $150 million target.

evidence.text: Gross profit for the second quarter of 2018 was $773.4 million , up $354.1 million , or 84.4% from $419.3 million from the corresponding quarter in 2017 . Our gross profit margins were 19.7% and 18.2% for the three month periods ended March 30, 2018 and March 31, 2017 , respectively.

factors.summary: The company has successfully completed the acquisition of CH2M, which is being integrated into its new business structure. The acquisition is contributing to revenue growth and market diversification, particularly in the water, nuclear, and environmental remediation sectors. The evidence highlights the company's strategic growth initiatives and the value created through cost savings and enhanced capabilities.

evidence.text: On December 15, 2017, the Company completed the acquisition of CH2M.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: For purposes of the Company’s second quarter fiscal 2018 segment reporting, the operating financial information of CH2M has been categorized within the Company’s new LOB business structure, with its sales and operating profit results for the time period during which CH2M has been under the ownership of the Company being allocated to the Company’s ATEN, BIAF and ECR lines of business.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: The purpose of the acquisition was to further diversify the Company’s market presence in the water, nuclear and environmental remediation sectors and to further the Company’s growth strategy.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: The Company paid total consideration of approximately $1.8 billion in cash (excluding $315.2 million of cash acquired) and issued approximately $1.4 billion of Jacobs’ common stock to the former stockholders and certain equity award holders of CH2M.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successful acquisition of CH2M contributing to growth and diversification.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is showing mixed trends, with manageable levels due to refinancing at lower rates and strong credit facilities, but also increasing due to higher average debt balances from financing activities related to the CH2M acquisition.

evidence.text: The Company had approximately $240.9 million in cash and cash equivalents held in the U.S. and $594.5 million held outside of the U.S. The Company had $449.2 million in letters of credit outstanding at March 30, 2018. Of this amount, $2.5 million was issued under the Revolving Credit Facility and $446.7 million was issued under separate, committed and uncommitted letter-of-credit facilities.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: The increases in net interest expense as compared to the corresponding periods last year was due primarily to higher levels of average debt balances outstanding related to financing activities for the acquisition of CH2M, which was partially funded with term loan financing of $1.5 billion and revolving credit line borrowings of $850 million.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Debt leverage shows mixed trends with both positive and negative factors, making it unclear overall.
